chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.750 of 2026 (Sher Muhammad vs. The State) For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 31.03.2026 Mr.Athar Hussain, advocate for applicant Ms. Firdous Faridi, Special Prosecutor, Custom a/w IO Saqib Sheryar, Preventive Officer, Custom O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.P-34/2025-IMO, 6/9 8, 14, 15 of CNS Act, 1997 (as amended in 2022), registered at Police Station Custom Airport JIAP, Karachi, by means of this application. 2. On 09.12.2025 at 11:00 hours, at International Mail Office, ICG Shed, Jinnah International Airport Customs, Karachi, a suspected parcel was received, which on examination was found containing 10226 MDMA tablets weighing 4.280 kilograms, hence, an FIR was registered. Allegedly on 19.12.2025, co-accused Kamran came to the said office and inquired about the impugned parcel, hence, he was arrested. In the course of investigation, he disclosed that he was directed by his friend Naeem to make an inquiry regarding arrival of the parcel from the said office. On such disclosure, communication between said accused and applicant was established in which applicant Sher Muhammad was identified to be the person whose name, and address and contact number were mentioned on the parcel, and he was its recipient. 3. Learned counsel for the applicant has pleaded for bail stating that applicant is innocent and the name of the applicant is not mentioned in the FIR and he has been falsely implicated in this case without any incriminating evidence. 4. On the other hand, learned Special Prosecutor, Custom has opposed bail, so also, IO of the case. 5.

Learned counsel for the applicant has pleaded for bail stating that applicant is innocent and the name of the applicant is not mentioned in the FIR and he has been falsely implicated in this case without any incriminating evidence. 4. On the other hand, learned Special Prosecutor, Custom has opposed bail, so also, IO of the case. 5. We have heard the parties and perused material available on record, which shows that on pointation of co-accused Kamran, applicant was traced out and in the investigation, he was identified to be the same person whose name, address and contact number were found present on the parcel, which he was to receive. Then, by using co-accused Kamran as a decoy, the Custom Officials succeeded in apprehending applicant and in further investigation, he transpired to be the owner of the impugned parcel. Such bits 2 and pieces collected in the investigation show prima facie involvement of applicant. 6. Therefore, in our view, there are sufficient grounds to believe prima facie connection of the applicant with the impugned parcel, and he is not entitled to grant of bail. This bail application is accordingly dismissed. However, learned trial court is directed to expedite the trial and conclude the same within a period of 03 months, after which, in any case, the applicant would be at liberty to repeat application for the same relief, which, if filed, shall be decided on its own merits. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA
